purpose: Permanent image model selection policy for long-running dark fantasy lore production.

do_not_use_for: scenes where forbidden objects repeatedly appear
observed_failures: weak obedience on exact hard action beats
drifted on child hand drawing symbol
missed some very specific scene blocking

do_not_use_for: current hard close-up symbol drawing scenes
scene 7 style tests until further evidence
observed_strengths: Leonardo model list describes it as precise and controllable
observed_failures: failed to produce the child hand drawing scene
drifted toward empty room and object/pyramid-like composition

use_for: one-off tests for hard action beats
hand/action scenes when SDXL repeatedly fails
do_not_use_for: automatic full-batch generation without visual review
style replacement for the whole channel without approval
observed_strengths: hit the hand-drawing-symbol beat better than SDXL and FLUX
observed_failures: may drift into graphic novel or comic-like rendering
requires style consistency review before approval

selection_rules: Use SDXL 1.0 for standard atmospheric scenes unless a better approved model is documented.
Do not use failed model tests as production assets.
Do not switch a video or channel style globally based on one good image.
High-risk scenes require one-image model testing before full regeneration.
A model can only be promoted after human review approves both beat match and channel style consistency.
Every generated manifest must store model_id, model_name if available, compiled_prompt, negative_prompt, beat_lock_used, and channel_style_used.
Never burn credits repeatedly on the same failed prompt and same model without changing the strategy.

must_pass: no watermark, stamp, logo, or fake text artifact

long_running_rule: This policy exists because Ember & Stone is intended to run indefinitely. Model selection must protect long-term quality, cost control, visual consistency, and recovery from failed generations.
